sources.writer_profile: Travel writer commissioned by National Geographic for the magazine's Best of the World 2026 feature, which named Rabat one of the world's top travel destinations for 2026. Her connection to Rabat is through a major commissioned feature piece. Editorial stance: National Geographic independent journalism with no advertising relationships.

Staff journalist at TelQuel, Morocco's leading independent French-language weekly newsmagazine based in Casablanca. Her July 2025 article is a summer lifestyle guide covering dining options for Moroccans staying in Casablanca and Rabat. Morocco-based with regular lifestyle and culture coverage. Editorial stance: TelQuel is known for independent, critical journalism in Morocco.

Travel blogger who writes exclusively about Morocco on maroclovers.com under the She Made the Trip brand. Her Rabat article includes specific MAD price ranges and named dishes suggesting personal visits. First name only; Morocco-focused editorial stance with no obvious advertising content. Note: publication date confirmed by metadata (2025-07-31) but not visible in article body text.

restaurants.name: Marea
restaurants.signature_dish: Fish fry; John Dory with basil and black olive sauce; sea bream on mushroom and spinach
restaurants.quote: Modern, Mediterranean-inspired restaurant specializing in fresh fish like John Dory with basil and black olive sauce, or sea bream prepared on mushroom and spinach.

restaurants.name: Dar Zaki
restaurants.signature_dish: Sweet-and-savory pastilla; couscous; tagine with chicken and candied apricots or lamb shanks with figs
restaurants.quote: Traditional Moroccan cuisine with creative twists—sweet-and-savory pastillas, classic fluffy couscous, and tagines perhaps with chicken with candied apricots or fall-apart tender lamb shanks with figs, served in a restored riad in the heart of the medina.

restaurants.name: Golden Fish
restaurants.quote: A real explosion of flavours and freshness, featuring salmon confit with citrus butter in Souissi Ambassadeurs.

restaurants.name: Le Grill Robuchon
restaurants.signature_dish: Tomato tartare; smoked meat skewers; peanut pie
restaurants.quote: Noted for divine service and perfect seasoning, featuring tomato tartare, smoked meat skewers, and peanut pie in Agdal.

restaurants.name: Le Laurier
restaurants.quote: Excellent pasta with seafood linguine, truffle pizzas, and tiramisu, with a crunchy salad to start, in Hassan's historic center.

restaurants.name: Le Relais de Paris
restaurants.signature_dish: Sole meunière; entrecôte with shallot sauce
restaurants.quote: Typical Parisian bistro with Belle Époque decor serving sole meunière and entrecôte with shallot sauce in Hassan.

restaurants.name: Sufra
restaurants.signature_dish: Lamb maqluba; tabbouleh; stuffed vine leaves; kunafah
restaurants.quote: Palestinian eatery serving tangy tabbouleh, stuffed vine leaves, and lamb maqluba layered with rice and vegetables, with Middle Eastern desserts like kunafah.

restaurants.name: Tangerino
restaurants.signature_dish: Patatas bravas; Ibérico ham; seafood paella
restaurants.quote: Stylish restaurant in upscale Souissi featuring authentic Spanish flavors from tapas including patatas bravas, Ibérico ham, and seafood paella, plus sangria and cocktails.

restaurants.name: Ty Potes
restaurants.signature_dish: Breton galettes; cheese and charcuterie; sweet crêpes
restaurants.quote: French bistro in a 1930s house offering Breton galettes, cheese, charcuterie, and sweet crêpes with garden seating.

restaurants.name: Villa Mandarine
restaurants.signature_dish: Grilled fish; market salads; homemade pastries
restaurants.quote: A peaceful institution at the heart of a citrus garden; a refined table away from the commotion to enjoy grilled fish, market salads, and homemade pastries in a timeless setting.
